name: Quality Benchmarking Dashboards [Minimal]
description: Demonstrates data sharing practices designed to support continuous improvement of clinical practice through quality benchmarking.
justification:
This task will demonstrate a use case of **data sharing for the continuous improvement of clinical practice**.

The initiative addresses healthcare consistency by establishing a unified platform that allows hospitals to benchmark their practices against evidence-based data and each other.

The dashboard aims to provide real-time, customisable data access, potentially supporting evidence-based decision-making while enabling quality control and improvement for various users and settings.

- **T6.5.1** — Identify clinically meaningful scenarios from scientific literature and clinical data, including time-critical therapy, emergency response times, pain management, and medical error detection.
- **T6.5.2** — Develop automated data processing pipelines using machine-readable transactions based on ETL processes (WP2).
- **T6.5.3** — Create a user-facing dashboard application utilizing infrastructure for automated data visualization comparing pan-European clinical benchmarks to local patient data.
- **T6.5.4** — Create APIs enabling data users to integrate clinical benchmark calculations into existing patient data management systems like Electronic Health Records.
- **T6.5.5** — Establish a consistent, anonymous benchmarking data set based on data disclosed through the federated infrastructure to reflect the heterogenic European population.
- **T6.5.6** — Generate a repository of machine-readable guideline recommendations for disclosure through the knowledge platform (WP5).

Led by **Falk von Dincklage** at Universitätsmedizin Greifswald.
